summary: Can't use `@export_tool_button` without reloading the editor

description.text:
### Tested versions

4.4beta4 and 4.4.rc1

### System information

Windows 10

### Issue description

Similar to #97834 but I'm not using the web editor. Whenever I try to use `@export_tool_button`, I'm always getting errors. I tried copying the code from the [documentation](https://docs.godotengine.org/en/latest/tutorials/scripting/gdscript/gdscript_exports.html#export-tool-button) to make sure I don't have code errors and I still get the same errors. Only after I've restarted or reloaded the project does the same code work. 

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d65d63cf-2aee-46a5-b137-ff235bdc201d)

### Steps to reproduce

As mentioned, I tested it with the same code as in the docs example. But I wanted to create a button to randomize the sprite of objects in my project. 

```gdscript
@export_tool_button("Randomize Sprite", "Callable") var rand_spr = randomize_sprite

func randomize_sprite() -> void:
	if sprite_configs.size() > 0: # sprite_configs contains Rect2 coords for regions of a texture
		var rng = randi() % sprite_configs.size()
		spr.region_rect = sprite_configs[rng]
``` 
This code works but if I make changes, I also have to restart/reload to make them work again.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A

summary: Annoying ghost letters left when switching chinese IME to english IME

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.3 stable, 4.4 rc1, 4.4.1 stable

### System information

Godot v4.4.rc1 - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1060 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6603) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-6300HQ CPU @ 2.30GHz (4 threads)

### Issue description

Maybe related to #96215. Using `shift+ctrl` or `win+space` to switch from Chinese IME to English IME during an unconfirmed composition will cause some annoying ghost letters left. No problem occurs when switching IMEs via mouse left-click.
**This issue occurs in all places in Godot.** I can't reproduce it outside of godot e.g. **notepad, GitHub text editor, webpage address or any other places**.
I only found two solutions to kill the annoying ghost letters. Clicking on other editable places or switching back to Chinese IME to reenter words. `Backspace` and `delete` don't work. The most frustrating part is that if you provide a TextEdit/LineEdit allowing players to input text, they may encounter this issue when using a Chinese IME.

_Edit:
Can't reproduce with Japanese IME.
Can't reproduce with Korean IME.
Test switching Chinese IME to Japanese/Korean IMEs can also reproduce this issue._

Annoying letters left:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/844c05f1-7705-4f2c-8f8d-e77a8acc0ec6)

**Reproducible**:

_Enabling sound will help understand the issue._

1. Script Editor

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/dc52e17e-362c-4175-80c1-09219eb054b4

2. Filters

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/860a9685-fa2b-4a52-81a0-26ffd8aadffe

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a7094b1f-1c24-4977-ab0d-30cce74e03d2

3. Control's Text

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/db312ad6-fdfe-46b4-b228-48ec337baba0

4. TextEdit Input

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b0bcde66-0354-4e79-bfb7-eedc922361ad

5. LineEdit Input

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/18c5490b-79b2-4391-a323-71a5e9f917a2

**Correct Behavior**:

_The correct behavior should be to cancel this input when switching._
 
1. Github

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/cd71fc32-9eca-4611-95d5-e50f7377a465

2. Address

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/429fd675-2ead-4531-bac4-ded6f6f2de15

### Steps to reproduce

1. Activate any Chinese IME e.g. Microsoft Pinyin IME is also able to reproduce the issue
2. Try to type some letters in wherever
3. Don't press `enter` or `shift` to confirm English words input. And also don't press `space` to confirm Chinese words input
4. Just use `shift+ctrl` or `win+space` to switch to English(or other languages) IME
5. You will see some annoying underlined ghost letters left

summary: AnimationPlayer subproperty keys are edited with wrong types [4.4]

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in v4.4.rc1.mono (Windows)
- Reproducible in v4.4.rc1 (Linux/Debian 12)
- Reproducible in v4.4.beta4 (Windows)

### System information

Godot v4.4.rc1.mono - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4050 Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6103) - AMD Ryzen 7 7840HS w/ Radeon 780M Graphics (16 threads)

### Issue description

In 4.4 you can now choose subnames/subproperties in a property track via popup. Previously you had to manually type :subname (`position:x`, for example) in track to animate them. But now the Engine is unable to recognize the correct type of such keys in the track. Whole types like` Vector2` (e.g. `position`) are detected correctly. But subproperties of these types (Like `x` or `y` of `Vector2`) are detected like types of their parent properties. (e.g. `position:x` is detected like `Vector2`, instead of `Float`). (See reproduction steps for pictures). _I thought this bug was already reported, but I was unable to find similar issues here. Sorry if this appears to be a duplicate._

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create an AnimationPlayer node
2. Create a new animation
3. Add new property track, choose any subproperty of any property, for example `position:x`:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/314b7274-8fe7-486a-93bb-fb517d79bd04)
4. Add a new keyframe.
Hovering over the keyframe tells us that the type of the keyframe is `float`:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5cacf4d1-aaae-4a95-b0f8-3ab2336b94e4)
but in the inspector it displays the keyframe like it has the `Vector2` type:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f6e38a72-5b58-4a08-928a-c738e7ec6fed)
5. Edit the value of the keyframe in the inspector: 
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a6b7e149-90b0-447b-a597-28defff52f7c)
6. Now the keyframe is marked as ❌ and tooltip of the keyframe says that its type is invalid: 
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/fd799833-24a4-4927-adf0-e59385fa276c)

description.text:

### System information

Godot v4.4.rc1 - Void #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Fri Feb 21 16:28:15 UTC 2025 on Wayland - Wayland display driver, Single-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - integrated Intel(R) Iris(R) Xe Graphics (ADL GT2) - 12th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-1240P (16 threads)

### Issue description

summary: Android 14: Export Template with changed minSdk results in pre-align errors

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.3.stable and 4.2.2.stable

### System information

Godot v4.3.stable - Linux Mint 21.3 (Virginia) - X11 - GLES3 (Compatibility)

### Issue description

Since Android 14 has an hard requirement for the minSdk version to be 23, I've compiled an export template where I've changed `platform/android/java/app/config.gradle`'s minSdk directly and compiled the export templates as such with both arm32 and arm64.

After trying to export the game **without** `Gradle Build` option, the resulting APK is not installable on my Android 14 device. The export settings are the following:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e3eca188-5f06-4e32-90c8-caf8bccebed6)

The error on `adb install` can be seen below:
```
Performing Incremental Install
Serving...
Failure [INSTALL_FAILED_INTERNAL_ERROR: INSTALL_FAILED_INTERNAL_ERROR: Failed to extract native libraries, res=-110]
Performing Streamed Install
adb: failed to install test.apk: Failure [INSTALL_FAILED_INVALID_APK: INSTALL_FAILED_INVALID_APK: Failed to extract native libraries, res=-2]
```

With the following logcat error:
```
PackageInstallerSession: START COMMIT SESSION: id{112768871}
NativeLibraryHelper: Library 'libgodot_android.so' is not page-aligned - will not be able to open it directly from apk.
NativeHelper: Failure copying native libraries [errorCode=-2]
PackageInstallerSession: Marking session 112768871 as failed: INSTALL_FAILED_INVALID_APK: Failed to extract native libraries, res=-2
PackageInstallerSession: Failed to verify session 112768871
PackageInstallerSession: result of session: -2{112768871}, INSTALL_FAILED_INVALID_APK: Failed to extract native libraries, res=-2
```

After looking around the issues I found a similar problem reported for Godot 3.2.4 in issue #42792, which took me to the `zipalign` and allowed me to check the resulting APK with `zipalign` and re-align it. This however revokes the signature and you will have to sign it again afterwards.

For now the workaround is to pre-align the libs and re-sign with:
```
# Export APK in Godot
zipalign -p -f 4 test.apk test-aligned.apk
apksigner sign --ks ... --ks-pass ... --ks-key-alias ... test-aligned.apk
```

EDIT:
~~Need to confirm before being sure, but for Android 14, the alignment might be required to be 8 bytes instead of 4 bytes, which would result in the zipalign command being `zipalign -p -f 8 test.apk test-aligned.apk` instead.~~
Seems to not be necessary after all.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Compile an export template for Android with an higher minSdk on `platform/android/java/app/config.gradle` and both arm32 and arm64
2. Export a game for Android **without** Gradle Build, using that compiled export
3. Attempt to install the resulting game in an Android 14 device

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

summary: InputEvent.is_action_pressed() doesn't detect both shift keys held on Linux

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in v4.4.rc.custom_build [394508d26]

### System information

Godot v4.4.rc (394508d26) - Fedora Linux 41 (Workstation Edition) on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - integrated Intel(R) Iris(R) Xe Graphics (TGL GT2) - 11th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-1185G7 @ 3.00GHz (8 threads)

### Issue description

`InputEvent.is_action_{pressed,released}()` with exact matching returns false when you push both shift keys, even though the action in the input map has specified left/right key locations. This works on Windows as a similar bug was fixed by https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/101763 but this issue remains.

Rewriting the _input function to not use is_action_pressed() like below, the problem doesn't occur, so it seems like it's a problem in the action matching code and not the key location detection.

```gdscript
# This works
func _input(event: InputEvent) -> void:
	if event is not InputEventKey || event.keycode != KEY_SHIFT:
		return
	if event.is_pressed():
		if event.location == KEY_LOCATION_LEFT:
			left.modulate = Color.RED
		if event.location == KEY_LOCATION_RIGHT:
			right.modulate = Color.RED
	else:
		if event.location == KEY_LOCATION_LEFT:
			left.modulate = Color.WHITE
		if event.location == KEY_LOCATION_RIGHT:
			right.modulate = Color.WHITE
```

### Steps to reproduce

1. Run the MRP on Linux
2. Push both shift keys
3. Notice the robot faces don't both turn red

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Using the MRP from https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/101384

[Edit: Updated description to mention that exact matching is being used in the test.]

summary: [4.4RC1] Android: Leaving Rendering Device Driver Set To Auto Crashes Exported Project

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.4RC1.
- Not Reproducible in 4.4 beta 4 and before.

### System information

Android 13 - Godot v4.4rc1 - Mobile - Qualcomm Adreno 650

### Issue description

PR  #103026 added the option to select which rendering driver to be used for each platform. They also added a new default setting which is `auto`. Currently, leaving the setting for `rendering/rendering_device/driver.android` to `auto` causes debug exported projects to crash. Changing the setting to `vulkan` fixes the issue.

Possibly relevant crash log:
```
02-22 18:49:21.266 19681 19720 E godot   : ERROR: Parameter "native_window" is null.
02-22 18:49:21.266 19681 19720 E godot   :    at: DisplayServerAndroid (platform/android/display_server_android.cpp:700)
02-22 18:49:21.266 19681 19720 E godot   : ERROR: Unable to create DisplayServer, all display drivers failed.
02-22 18:49:21.266 19681 19720 E godot   : Use "--headless" command line argument to run the engine in headless mode if this is desired (e.g. for continuous integration).
02-22 18:49:21.266 19681 19720 E godot   :    at: setup2 (main/main.cpp:3015)
```

Additional concerns: Someone should probably check exports on iOS and MacOS as those are probably just as overlooked as Android exports.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a simple, exportable project for Android with default Project Settings.
2. Export project to Android.
3. Run project and watch it crash.

Extra steps to fix:
4. Change `rendering/rendering_device/driver.android` in Project Settings to `vulkan`.
5. Export and run project to see it work as normal.

summary: Viewport feedback color degradation

description.text: ### Tested versions 4.0, 4.1, 4.4 beta 4 ### System information Godot v4.4.beta4 - macOS Sequoia (15.3.0) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - Apple M2 - Apple M2 (8 threads) ### Issue description Two SubViewports nested in a feedback loop do not preserve the original color. Specifically, when the Viewports employ 3D rendering and the project is on the Compatibility renderer. It most likely means that all 3D rendering is ever so slightly off color. Here's a video. Note the color ramp moving downward to show the degradation over time. Interesting how it remains monochrome - each RGB value is affected equally https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0adab120-8eb0-496f-bc4a-a9760c887c17 What it should look like. Captured on Forward+ I have a custom Node called [Feedback](https://github.com/celyk/godot-useful-stuff/blob/main/scripts/Feedback.gd) that exhibits the problem, as it is just 2 Viewports under the hood. I have been using Feedback on iOS with limited success, but the color shifting is very annoying and destroys certain effects. summary: ShapeCast2D doesn't report collisions even though safe fraction < 1.0

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in:
- v4.3.stable.official [77dcf97d8]
- v4.4.dev7.official [46c8f8c5c]

### System information

Godot v4.3.stable - Ubuntu 24.10 24.10 - Wayland - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4080 (nvidia; 560.35.03) - AMD Ryzen 7 7800X3D 8-Core Processor (16 Threads)

### Issue description

In some cases `ShapeCast2D` does not report any collisions even though both - safe and unsafe fractions are below `1.0`. It is most likely caused by some floating-point arithmetic errors as it seems to occur only when positions are big enough and motion/penetration are low enough. Increasing motion vector slightly makes the issue to not occur.
But we are not talking about some enormous numbers. It is easily reproducible with penetration size being 0.75px and positions only in thousands pixels. We should rather not encounter such errors in this scale. Also returned value should always be consistent and safe fraction should never be lower than 1.0 if there are no collisions.

P.S. Additional question. Is it expected that `collision_result` returns only collisions in the closest point of impact instead of returning all the collisions along whole motion vector? It would be definitely much more useful if it returned all the collisions along whole motion.

### Steps to reproduce

- Add `StaticBody2D` at some big position (like 8000x8000)
- Add `ShapeCast2D` just above static body - 0.25px above
- Set `ShapeCast2D.target_position` to `Vector2.DOWN` (`(0, 1)`)

Unsafe fraction is `0.25` as expected but no collisions are reported.
However increasing `target_position` slightly by 0.01 makes it work again.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[shapecast2d-bug.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18905137/shapecast2d-bug.zip)

summary: Translation CSV importer produces invalid or duplicate UIDs

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in all 4.4.beta (tested in 4.4beta4)
- Not present in 4.3 or earlier.

### System information

Godot v4.4.beta4 - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Single-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3090 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6094) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-9900K CPU @ 3.60GHz (16 threads)

### Issue description

Two bugs with the translation csv importer. Both of these are regression in Godot 4.4 due to the new consistent UID hashing added in PR #97363

First, it can produce negative UIDs, which will serialize as `uid://<invalid>`. Negative UIDs are currently not permitted for text serialization, though they work fine in binary serialization.

Second, it does not check if the UID already exists in the project. This causes a few glitches which are hard to verify, for example referencing one file may reference the wrong copy (it might be random which gets referenced due to the nature of duplicate UIDs in Godot).

Also, note that reproducing this in a fresh project will not be obvious, because Godot caches incorrect UIDs due to a bug with how `ResourceSaver::set_uid()` interacts with the editor's UID cache. You must close the editor and delete .godot to observe the new UIDs. This whole thing with set_uid probably belongs in its own issue.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open attached MRP.
2. Open `reference_duplicate_uid.tscn` and press ctrl-s to resave scene.
3. Open reference_duplicate_uid.tscn` in text editor and note that both `test_translation.es.translation` and `test_translation.es_1.translation` have the same UIDs
4. Open translation_ref.tscn and press ctrl-s to resave scene
5. Check `translation_ref.tscn` in text editor and note `uid://<invalid>` for `test_translation.en.translation`

Optional steps to demonstrate that the invalid only happens on .tscn but not .scn:
6. Open translation_ref.scn and press ctrl-s to resave scene. This will not produce an invalid uid, but we cannot read this in a text editor.
7. Rename .en.translation outside godot and then open both `translation_ref.scn` and `translation_ref.tscn` The <invalid> only occurs for .tscn because binary resources permit negative UIDs. The .scn version will update the reference correctly.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[translation_csv_uids.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18903965/translation_csv_uids.zip)

summary: Editor hangs when printing complex shader errors to the output panel

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in v4.3.stable.official [77dcf97d8], v4.4.beta4.official [93d270693]

### System information

Godot v4.4.beta4 - Fedora Linux 41 (Workstation Edition) on Wayland - X11 display driver, Single-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - AMD Radeon RX 560 Series (radeonsi, polaris11, LLVM 19.1.0, DRM 3.60, 6.12.15-200.fc41.x86_64) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-2500K CPU @ 3.30GHz (4 threads)

### Issue description

When the visibility of output messages (and, to a lesser degree, error messages) is enabled, it's easy to hang the editor for a long time by typing invalid code.

Shader includes, in particular, quickly bloat the output—especially when chained together (with `#ifndef` include guards). There's a lot of redundant and irrelevant content printed. An example chain might be a shader that uses a specialty include that use a utility function include that uses a math include. The attached project uses `shader > fog > colors > gradients > utilities > math` (although, in retrospect, `gradients > colors` would have been a more natural example relationship order). That chain is very similar to the chains I use in my current game project. And, pretty much everything uses my custom math include. So, editing that file prints _a lot_.

Ideal changes:

1. Don't print messages about shader errors to the output in the first place. Don't even waste CPU cycles generating the strings. In big projects, the editor hitching is frustrating. The output is worthless while working in the shader editor and a mess when viewed in the output panel.
2. If something _really_ needs printed, only print a minimal message that redirects the user to the file path so that they can see the error in the shader editor instead. And, even then, maybe put them in a separate output panel category.

### Steps to reproduce

This test was performed with `interface/editor/single_window_mode` enabled.

1. Open the attached project in Godot v4.4.beta4.official [93d270693].
2. Ensure that the output panel is set to print standard output and error messages.
3. Open `shaders/common/math.gdshaderinc` in the shader editor.
4. Type some invalid code (like "test") into `math.gdshaderinc`, to cause an error.
5. Observe that the editor becomes unresponsive for a long time. If not, duplicate `test_shader_1.gdshader` or `test_shader_2.gdshader` to make printing output take even longer.
6. Undo the error, restoring `math.gdshaderinc` to an error-free state.
7. In the output panel, disable the visibility of standard output and error messages.
8. Add an error to `math.gdshaderinc` again.
9. Observe that the editor becomes unresponsive for less time than when visibility of output messages was enabled.

Alt-tabbing on X11 is very slow when a Godot editor or project is open

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.0.stable, 4.3.stable, 4.4.beta 93d270693

### System information

Godot v4.4.beta (93d270693) - Fedora Linux 41 (KDE Plasma) on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4090 (nvidia; 565.77) - 13th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-13900K (32 threads)

### Issue description

Alt-tabbing on X11 is very slow when a Godot editor or project is open.

This occurs regardless of whether compositing is enabled or not, and affects all rendering methods (although Forward+/Mobile seems to be more heavily affected than Compatibility). This affects any project as long as it renders at a high enough framerate.

In this video, I start alt-tabbing as soon as the previous dialog is gone. Notice how alt-tabbing is much faster near the end of the video after I close the empty project:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7f79ece4-3370-487b-be32-1fa7414e5f0e

Some things that improve the situation:

- Disabling V-Sync (even if you cap FPS to the monitor refresh rate).
- Using a low **Max FPS** cap in the project settings like `10`.
- Enabling low-processor mode and ensuring nothing causes unnecessary redrawing in the project. (This is not viable in most games.)

I suspect X11 is getting spammed with API calls when Godot is open, which leads to these performance issues. I haven't tested this on Wayland yet.

Godot 3.x is also affected (and is less affected with low-processor mode or V-Sync off), but much less so than 4.x. It's affected to a similar rate as various games I've tested like Cube 2: Sauerbraten, so I expect this to be par of the course for KDE's task switcher.

Note that if you intend to test this locally, KDE's task switcher has an intentional delay that means it'll only appear after holding <kbd>Alt + Tab</kbd> for long enough. This is why it never appears instantly, but switching between the last two tasks can be done quickly as it doesn't need to spawn the task switcher dialog for this to happen. This is different to Windows's task switcher which appears instantly.


### Steps to reproduce

- Run any project and try to alt-tab on Linux/X11.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[test_empty_project.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18897018/test_empty_project.zip)

summary: Fix uninitialized value in Tonemap

description.text:
And anything that uses luminance.

The class Luminance in luminance.cpp is in charge of averaging the luminance of all pixels.
It performs multiple passes until it reaches a 1x1 texture containing the total average. This is standard luminance averaging on GPU.

Then the "result" of this frame and the "prev_frame_result" are averaged together at a certain speed to mimic eye adaptation.

Then this avarege becomes the "source" for the next frame. This is done here:

```cpp
SWAP(p_luminance_buffers->current,
p_luminance_buffers->reduce.write[p_luminance_buffers->reduce.size() -
1]);
```

So far pretty normal stuff.

**The problem is: prev_frame_result IS UNINITIALIZED**. Therefore it's possible for prev_frame_result to contain garbage values like -5+e15 which causes the screen to stay black for a minute until eye adaptation catches up.

Windows will always force allocations to be reset to 0, but Linux does not do that.
However Windows just delays the bug; because it's possible for VMA to reuse a block.

You can repro this bug by downloading Bistro, creating a camera, selecting a default scene; and then launching Bistro.

Everything will work fine.
Until you decide to resize the window. It takes a few tries on Godot, but eventually the screen becomes black.

If you wait around a minute, the screen will "unblack" itself back to normal.

Even if it's not stuck in black after resize, you may notice that every resize is inconsistent in how the eye adaptation catches up (i.e. sometimes it flashes to white, sometimes it does not).

If you can't repro the bug, you need to try harder by doing arbitrary resizes until it triggers.
Also, I advise to try this on Linux; since Windows' sanitization of memory gets in the way.

There's probably multiple tickets already filled around issues that were rooted in luminance calculations starting from uninitialized memory.

This PR sets a default value of 0, which causes the screen to always flash white after resize. Setting a different value like 0.1 makes the flash effect weaker. Setting it to a high value like 5.0 makes the screen flash from dark instead.

This bug can be backported to 4.3. I don't know if it can be backported to earlier; as the render graph makes sure the texture_clear() calls gets issued in the right place; whereas in <= 4.2 it might be problematic depending on when Luminance::LuminanceBuffers::configure is being called.

If there is more than one node selected in the SceneTree, `EditorPlugin::forward_canvas_gui_input` will only be called if `_handles` always returns true, regardless of which types of Nodes are being selected.

Even this implementation isn't enough for it to work: `func _handles(obj): return obj is Node`

Additionally, if there is no selection in the SceneTree, and you attempt to drag a node in the 2D viewport, you will be able to drag the node normally but the `InputEventMouseButton` that should have been called when pressing the mouse is not emitted unless `_handles` always returns true.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open the attached project.
2. Open the scene "example.tscn"
3. Select and drag (in the 2D viewport) any of the nodes "A"/"B" individually -> note that the editor plugin is emitting logs due to the correctly triggered events.
4. Select both "A"/"B" then drag (in the 2D viewport) them at the same time -> note that no logs are emitted.
5. Click outside the 2D viewport, then attempt to drag either "A" or "B" by clicking directly in the viewport (don't select them in the scene tree first) -> note that motion events are emitted, but the first expected MouseButton (pressing left button) event is not, only the release event is (eventually) emitted.
6. Open the script "res://addons/myplugin/myplugin.gd", then comment line 10 and uncomment line 11.
7. Repeat steps 3, 4 and 5 -> note that the results are the same.
8. In the myplugin.gd script, comment line 11 and uncomment line 12.
9. Repeat steps 3, 4, and 5 -> note that now both 3 and 4 steps are emitting logs, and the "Press event" in step 5 is also emitted.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[forward_canvas_gui_input-bug.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18888161/forward_canvas_gui_input-bug.zip)

summary: Bezier Curve tracks in an animation aren't played in the animation tree

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducable in v4.3.stable.official [77dcf97d8]

### System information

Godot v4.3.stable - Windows 10.0.19045 - Vulkan (Mobile) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1060 6GB (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6094) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-6600K CPU @ 3.50GHz (4 Threads)

### Issue description

Any bezier tracks I key, aren't played in the animation tree.
I animate a 3D node in my character. If I use a property track for animating the rotation, this works as expected.
If I instead create a bezier curve track for rotation, I can animate it, but that part isn't played in the animation tree.
Any other tracks in the animation that are non bezier tracks, play as expected.

In the image you first see the animation in the animation player. It rotates.
Then you see the animation in the animation tree where the rotation isn't processed.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c913d657-c43c-4e0f-9919-ed51c5729e00)
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f092f601-f535-45e3-9aca-6647241acfaa)

### Steps to reproduce

Create a player character. 
Inside that create a simple 3D node I call "Root_CharModel". Under that a simple capsule to see the animation.
Add an animation player and create an animation that animates the position with a simple property track.
Then create a bezier curve track for the rotation.
Create some animation.
Then create an animation tree state machine.
Add a state that simply plays your animation and in the preview it already doesn't work. The bezier curve tracks don't play.

summary: Add support for offscreen rendering

description.text:
Implement https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/5790
- Add `MODE_OFFSCREEN` `Mode` to `Window`.
- Adds `--offscreen` CLI flag, which use `MODE_OFFSCREEN` on the main window.
- `--resolution` change the resolution of the viewport if using `--offscreen` and the `stretch_mode` set to "viewport".
- Only work on Windows. It will probably just break or just not work if tried on other OS.
- Currently, it was made for OpenGL renderer. **Not tested**, but it should also work for Vulkan.
- It still creates a window, because OpenGL FrameBuffer Object cannot be used without it. However, the window is minimized and removed from the taskbar. For Vulkan, the window can be removed in the future.
- The offscreen viewport size is still depended on the window size, unless using the "viewport" stretch mode, of course.

## Sample Project

Sample project: [offscreentest.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18885733/offscreentest.zip)


(The project may need to be opened normally in-editor first for assets to be imported.)

This project has a scene with a rotating cube and some music. It saves screenshots at a set interval to the snaps folder (only up to 10 images should be saved, in png format).

Suggested methods of testing:

- `godot.exe --offscreen`
  - This uses the offscreen window mode and no other changes. Audio should still work. Snapshots should appear in the snaps folder.
- `godot.exe --offscreen --write-movie output.avi`
  - Uses the offscreen window mode, but also runs in movie mode. The resulting movie file should show the spinning cube.

## Implementation Notes
- It was implemented as `WindowMode`, so that all windows can independently be offscreen. Not sure if it's better to add it as a separate window option like `window.offscreen = false/true`, or just make it an option only for the main window.
- Hiding window from taskbar implementation is stolen from #99856

## To do
- Remove window when using offscreen on Vulkan.
- **Maybe** add support for other platform like Linux and macOS. (I don't have any of these OS, so it would be hard to test)
